title: VimFu — gV Avoid Reselect
description: gV prevents automatic reselection of the previous visual area when used in mappings. A subtle but useful mapping tool.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

setup.content:
# Selection Demo
alpha = 1
beta = 2
gamma = 3
delta = 4
epsilon = 5

steps.say: G shift V is used in mappings to prevent the previous visual area from being reselected. It's a mapping helper, not a standalone command.
When you run a colon command from visual mode, Vim can auto-reselect the visual area. G shift V prevents that. Let's see the difference.
Run a substitution. Colon S, alpha, omega.
Done. Now if a mapping were to reselect here, G shift V would prevent it. It clears the pending visual selection inside a mapping.
This is mainly useful when writing custom mappings that execute colon commands on visual selections. You add G shift V at the end of the mapping to prevent re-highlighting.
G shift V. A mapping tool to suppress visual reselection. Niche, but handy for clean custom visual mode mappings.
